text: This paper aims to reveal the functions and characteristics of the Pattani Central Mosque, a counter-narrative to the image of the mosque in the South being used as hotbeds of extremist Islamism and the breeding grounds for potential 'martyrs'. In light of the incident of the Kru-ze Mosque in 2004 which revealed the failure of religious institution to be protected from violence this paper investigates the Islamic education process and management of mosques as well as their views of democratic governance, human rights and counter-extremism which are conducted through in-depth interviews of Imams, preachers and mosque members. The research results have revealed that the Pattani Central Mosque is composed of moderate and Muslim extremists who do not lean heavily to extremist or radical schools of thought. This counter-narrative that is contrary to the dominant description that is used in politics for a representation of the mosque in the deep south.
